name: agent-velocity
installHints: AiPlus Agent Velocity adds AI-native time estimation: it tracks estimated vs actual time per task, learns model + task-type bias, and surfaces calibrated p50/p90 estimates over time.
Functionality is exposed via the built-in `aiplus velocity` subcommand. This module ships the schemas + design docs that runtimes can reference locally.
Run `aiplus velocity init` in a project to start tracking. Then `aiplus velocity estimate` before a task and `aiplus velocity complete` after.

notes: Public module record. Code lives in the AiPlus CLI (subcommand `aiplus velocity`).
Schemas + design docs + the run-record / estimate-record / config / rare-case-record JSONL shapes are public; per-project velocity data lives under `.aiplus/velocity/` and is not bundled.

Velocity records only estimated vs actual durations, model name, task-type, workflow level, and outcome (pass / needs_fix / blocked). No prompt text, transcript text, file contents, or secret values are ever recorded.
Records carry timestamps and a hashed task-id only — no PII beyond what the user types into `--task` / `--task-type`.
Extracted from AiPlus's existing `velocity` subcommand as a sibling public module.
v0.1 documents the existing feature; the Rust source remains in `aiplus-core` for now and may be split into its own crate later.
